title: ficus macrophylla f. macrophylla
intro: Ficus macrophylla f. macrophylla, also known as the Moreton Bay fig, is a large evergreen tree native to Australia, characterized by its broad, spreading canopy and edible fruit. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of the Moreton Bay fig have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used in poultices to treat skin conditions and wounds.
